summary: Comprehensive backend API testing completed for Washop marketplace platform. Tested 55 endpoints across authentication, vendor management, products, orders, reviews, access keys, wishlist, claims, search, notifications, admin dashboard, and flash sales. Overall success rate: 92.7% (51/55 tests passed).

critical_bugs.issue: TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes
critical_bugs.impact: Prevents vendors from activating subscription keys
critical_bugs.details: Line 100 in access_keys.py compares timezone-naive datetime with timezone-aware datetime

api_design_issues.issue: Expects status as query parameter but test sends in request body
api_design_issues.details: Endpoint signature: update_order_status(order_id: str, status: OrderStatus, user=Depends(get_current_user))

api_design_issues.issue: Expects employee_id as query parameter but test sends in request body
api_design_issues.details: Endpoint signature: assign_claim(claim_id: str, employee_id: str, user=Depends(role_required('admin')))

auth_issues.issue: Refresh endpoint expects refresh token but test sends access token
auth_issues.details: Test implementation issue - need to use refresh token from cookies or separate refresh token

passed_tests: User registration with role-based vendor profile creation
Product CRUD operations with vendor quotas
Order creation with WhatsApp URL generation
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix timezone comparison bug in access_keys.py line 100. Consider standardizing API parameter passing (query vs body) for consistency. The datetime comparison issue is blocking subscription key activation which is a core business function.
